reasoning: The FRED statistical tool returned a downward trend over the previous year (-0.0012 per day), culminating in a recent uptick from 1.554 to 1.608 in November 2025. Evaluating against an anchor of 0.30 given by the analytical model, we expect mean-reversion and continuation of the medium-term downward drift to keep the probability of a further increase low over the near term. However, October 2025 polls note expectations for nominal 10-year Treasury yields to rise slightly (to 4.10% from ~4.08%), which prevents real yields from dropping steeply and pushes our estimate up slightly from the strict model output. Uncertainty increases with the forecast horizon, pulling the probability closer to 0.5 for the later resolution date.
